title: DNA isolation from cattle semen  for long read sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ehjk","text":"Here we describe a method for isolate high molecular weight DNA from commercially available frozen bull semen straws.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"37st0","text":"This protocol is based on a salting-out method and uses several commercially available solutions. It consists of several steps: washing of semen, lysis, removal of proteins and precipitation of genomic DNA.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"14scp","text":"This protocol was used to isolate DNA from sixty semen straws, all of which were successfully sequenced using the CLR sequencing mode on the PacBio SequelII platform.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eiprt","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aasce","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"69mfv","text":"Salting out is a good method to obtain high molecular weight (HMW) DNA, as it avoids damaging steps such as the use of purification columns or heavy mixing with phenol/chloroform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"735jn","text":"Note that all mixing steps should be gentle to obtain HMW DNA fragments (from lysis steps to DNA precipitation). We also recommend to use DNA low bind tubes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"15djg","text":"As we use commercial sperm straws to perform our extractions, we do not always know the composition of these straws, the quantity of material contained, the nature of the diluents and preservatives used. This is why it is sometimes necessary to use several straws to obtain enough material for sequencing. It is also sometimes wise to perform several washes (see step 3) to eliminate contaminants from diluents and preservatives.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6lhi8","text":"See Safety Data Sheets for warnings and safety hazards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Recovery of spermatozoa from the straw: - Empty the 200 µL Sample in a 2 mL, tube by cutting the two ends of the straw DNA LoBind Tubes 2.0 mL Eppendorf Catalog #30108078 - Rince the straw it with 200 µL, 1X PBS Room temperature Phosphate-buffered saline, pH 7.4 Contributed by users
Step one: Add 500 µL of RLT-TCEP to the pellet - Vortex 00:00:10 by pulsing at max speed - If necessary, use a wide opening tip to resuspend the pellet - Incubate On ice 00:10:00

Wash: - Add 800 µL more PBS (up to 1 mL, 1X PBS ) - Pellet 1000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes - Discard the supernatant Second wash is optional (no significant impact observed) -Re-suspend in 1 mL, 1X PBS -Pellet 1000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes -Discard the supernatant Centrifuge gently so that the pellet does not stick. It should be easy to resuspend for efficient lysis.

Step two: continue with Qiagen Puregene Tissue kit adapted as follow - Add 500 µL of Cell Lysis Solution - Add 60 µL of 20 mg/mL proteinase K (20 mg/ml) - Mix by inversion ( about 25 inversions) - Incubate 55 °C 01:30:00 Puregene Tissue Kit Qiagen Catalog #158063 Proteinase K Qiagen Catalog #19133 (CAS: 39450-01-6)
- Add 200 µL of Protein precipitation buffer (from Qiagen Puregene Tissue Kit) - Mix by hand or gently vortexing 00:00:15 - Incubate On ice 00:05:00 - Centrifuge 16000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
Remove RNA : - 3 µL RNAse from Qiagen Puregene Tissue Kit - Incubate 37 °C 00:15:00 - Incubate On ice 00:01:00

Immidiately before use, prepare a mix containing RLT buffer (Qiagen) and TCEP [Tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine hydrochloride] to a final volume of 500µL per sample as follow: - 450 µL, RTL - 50 µL, TCEP Buffer RLT Qiagen Catalog #79216 Tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine hydrochloride solution Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #646547-10X1ML This mixture of a guanidine-based reagent (RLT) and a thiol-free reducing agent facilitate dissociation of disulfide bonds (Wu et al , 2018). TCEP is odorless, and more stable than DTT (Han & Han, 1994). A Procedure for Quantitative Determination of Tris(2-Carboxyethyl)phosphine, an Odorless Reducing Agent More Stable and Effective Than Dithiothreitol. Analytical Biochemistry. Rapid method for the isolation of mammalian sperm DNA..

- Add 600 µL of 70% ethanol to the pellet - Centrifuge 5000 x g, °C, 2 minutes - Discard supernatant - Almost dry the pellet Room temperature 00:05:00 - Add 50 µL to 100 µL of EB (Qiagen) or TE buffer to eluate DNA - Store DNA at 4°C EB buffer Qiagen Catalog #19086 DNA in EB buffer can be heated to 60°C for 1 hour to dissolve it. Do not vortex or pipet DNA. It is recommended not to freeze the DNA to preserve long fragments. Of the 60 extractions carried out using this protocol, the average size of the fragments generated is around 53 kb, ranging from 25 to 120 kb on average. We expect 30 ug of DNA from a commercial semen straw, but this figure can vary considerably from sample to sample. We obtained absorbance ratios of 260/280 for DNA of around 1.8 nm, and 260/230 ratios averaging 0.5 nm. Low ratios have already been observed using RLT buffer (Wu et al , 2018), but these did not affect PacBio sequencing significantly. Some of these DNA have been sequenced and published in Jourdain et al. 2023. Large-scale detection and characterization of interchromosomal rearrangements in normozoospermic bulls using massive genotype and phenotype data sets..

- Transfert the supernatant to a new tube containing 600 µL of Isopropanol - Carrefully invert the tube 25-50X times to form the pellet - Incubate 00:05:00 Room temperature - Centrifuge 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute - Discard supernatant Isopropanol Contributed by users
creator: Carole Iampietro
